title: Liquidity values are inverted for USDm-base pools
reasoning: The component explicitly says USDm-base pools require the existing oracle parser to invert the raw feed direction, but then bypasses that parser and always uses oraclePrice / 1e24. For a USDm-base pool, the non-USDm reserve is multiplied by the inverse of the needed USD value, so the two reserve-value series are wrong by roughly price^2 relative to each other. This undermines the chart's stated invariant that a balanced pool should produce overlapping lines.
reproduction: Render LiquidityChart with token0Symbol="USDm", token1Symbol="CELO", oraclePrice="2000000000000000000000000" and equal USD value reserves. The chart multiplies CELO amount by 2 instead of the inverted USDm-base value expected by the existing parser contract described in the component comment, so the lines do not overlap for a balanced pool.
recommendation: Use the same canonical oracle-price conversion helper used elsewhere for pool display, or branch on USDm-base direction and invert the raw value before multiplying the non-USDm reserve. Keep the feed-direction handling in one shared helper so charts and summary metrics cannot drift.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise unrelated address-label, breach-history, and breaker components. None render LiquidityChart or assert USD conversion for USDm-base versus USDm-quote pools.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a LiquidityChart unit test with react-plotly.js mocked to capture props. Cover both token0Symbol="USDm" and token1Symbol="USDm" with equivalent reserve values and assert the plotted y arrays overlap when converted to USD.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/components/liquidity-chart.tsx plus a focused component test for the two feed directions.

history.note: LiquidityChart now uses parseOraclePriceToNumber for canonical USDm-base inversion and has a Plotly-prop regression for USDm-base/quote balanced reserves.
